    • Stage 2 — Holocene temperature reconstruction
  • Reference

Simulate racemization for a single downcore sample (with burial damping)

Source: R/kinetics.R
racemizeSample.Rd

Full pipeline for one sample: selects the portion of the temperature history from deposition to present, applies dampSedTemps to attenuate the seasonal signal with burial depth, then integrates racemization via racemize.

Usage

racemizeSample(
  sampleAge,
  ageVec,
  sumT,
  winT,
  x,
  dT,
  startingDL = 0,
  scenario = NA,
  startDamp = 1,
  endDamp = 0.5,
  dampTime = 10,
  ...
)

Arguments

sampleAge

Age of the sample (ka BP). Only temperature history at or older than this age is used.

ageVec

Numeric vector of ages spanning the full temperature history (ka BP).

sumT

Numeric vector of summer half-year temperatures (°C), same length as ageVec.

winT

Numeric vector of winter half-year temperatures (°C), same length as ageVec.

x

Power-law exponent.

dT

Timestep in years.

startingDL

Starting D/L value. Default 0.

scenario

Character label for the output. Default NA.

startDamp, endDamp, dampTime

Passed to dampSedTemps.

...

Additional arguments passed to arrhenius.

Value

A one-row data frame with columns age, DL, and scenario.

See also

racemize, dampSedTemps, racemize_depth_series for the more physically rigorous depth-series workflow.

Examples

age  <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.01)
sumT <- rep(15, length(age))
winT <- rep(4,  length(age))
racemizeSample(sampleAge = 5, ageVec = age, sumT = sumT, winT = winT,
               x = 3, dT = 10)
#>   age        DL scenario
#> 1   5 0.1780704       NA
